The director has five scenes with shooting days 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively and the release years 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015 corresponding to decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}  
The constraint requires no two consecutive scenes to be from the same decade  
To minimize total shooting days we need to arrange scenes in a sequence that alternates decades  
One possible sequence is 1994 2004 2012 2007 2015 which corresponds to decades {1990-1999} {2000-2009} {2010-2019} {2000-2009} {2010-2019}  
This sequence satisfies the constraint as no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade  
The total shooting days are 4 + 6 + 12 + 8 + 10 = 40 days  
The sequence can be optimized further by trying different arrangements but the minimum total days is 40  
The cost per day for scenes are 1 1.5 2 2.5 3 lakhs respectively  
The total budget is 50 lakhs  
We need to maximize the number of scenes completed without exceeding the budget  
Let x1 x2 x3 x4 x5 be the number of days each scene is shot  
The objective is to maximize the number of scenes completed which is the sum of binary variables indicating if a scene is completed  
Subject to the constraint that the total cost is less than or equal to 50 lakhs  
The linear programming problem is formulated with these constraints  
The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 4
